## Summary

What changed, why is it needed, and what user or system outcome does it produce?

## Change class

Select one:

- [ ] Class 0: Administrative or documentation
- [ ] Class 1: Standard implementation
- [ ] Class 2: Verifier-critical
- [ ] Class 3: Release, migration, or security-sensitive

Affected trust boundary:

## TDD evidence

- [ ] Acceptance criteria were defined before implementation
- [ ] A focused test or formal obligation failed first for the expected reason
- [ ] The smallest coherent implementation made it pass
- [ ] Refactoring preserved passing behavior
- [ ] Relevant unit and integration suites pass
- [ ] Failure paths and boundary cases are covered
- [ ] Documentation-only exception applies and is explained below

Red evidence:

Green evidence:

Commands and results:

## Mathematical verification

- [ ] No mathematical trust boundary is affected
- [ ] Candidate output was checked independently from its generator
- [ ] Formal proof passes the pinned kernel or checker
- [ ] Counterexample premises and violated conclusion were checked
- [ ] Assumptions, domains, tolerances, and dependency pins are recorded
- [ ] Claim, theorem, witness, or artifact identifiers are linked

Verifier and result:

## Provenance and licensing

- [ ] No new third-party code, data, model output, or dependency
- [ ] New third-party material has source, license, and attribution recorded
- [ ] Generated artifacts include tool, version, configuration, seed, and hash as applicable
- [ ] MathCorpus and RL export implications are documented
- [ ] No credentials, private data, or access-controlled material are included

Provenance references:

## Risk and recovery

Known limitations:

Rollback or recovery plan:

Follow-up work:

## Review readiness

- [ ] The branch is current with main
- [ ] The pull request addresses one coherent concern
- [ ] Required checks pass
- [ ] Review threads are resolved
- [ ] I have not represented generated output as independently verified evidence
